question: Are there exactly seven rock specimens visible in the image?
Are the rock specimens arranged in two distinct vertical columns?
Does the left column contain exactly three specimens (Mafic series)?
Does the right column contain exactly four specimens (Felsic series)?
Does the top-left specimen (Gabbro) exhibit a coarse-grained crystalline texture and dark color?
Does the middle-left specimen (Basalt) exhibit a fine-grained texture and dark color?
Does the bottom-left specimen (Basaltic Scoria) display a highly vesicular (porous/hole-filled) texture?
Does the top-right specimen (Granite) show coarse-grained visible crystals and a lighter color (pink/gray/white)?
Does the second specimen in the right column (Rhyolite) exhibit a fine-grained texture consistent with rapid cooling?
Does the third specimen in the right column (Obsidian) display a smooth, black, glassy texture?
Does the bottom-right specimen (Pumice) appear highly porous/frothy and light-colored?
Excluding the obsidian, is the left column (Mafic) generally darker in tone than the right column (Felsic)?
Do the textures in the left column transition from coarse-grained (top) to porous (bottom), reflecting increasing cooling rates?
Is the surface beneath the specimens a black slate countertop?
Is the lighting uniform and shadowless, consistent with a ring light or light box setup?
Is the image free of any visible text, labels, or watermarks?
Are all rock specimens fully visible without being cropped out of the frame?
